question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

shellCommands: "C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command 'rg -n "fn timer_with_threaded_runtime|fn timer_with_current_thread_scheduler|fn tickspace|fn starving|fn timeout_value" tokio/tests/time_rt.rs'

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"fn schedule\\(|fn shutdown\\(|fn shutdown_immediately\\(|fn create_shutdown1|fn create_drop1|fn spawn_niche_in_task\" tokio/src/runtime/tests/task.rs"
"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command 'rg -n "fn fits_256_one_at_a_time|fn overflow|fn steal_batch|fn stress1|fn stress2" tokio/src/runtime/tests/queue.rs'

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"pub fn spawn<F>|pub fn spawn_blocking<F, R>|pub fn block_on<F: Future>|pub fn enter\\(|pub fn handle\\(|pub fn new\\(\" tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"pub fn spawn<F>|pub(crate) fn spawn_named|pub fn block_on<F: Future>|fn block_on_inner|pub fn enter\\(\" tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"fn schedule\\(&self, task: task::Notified<Self>\\)|fn schedule\\(&self, task: Notified<Self>\\)\" tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/handle.rs"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command 'rg -n "pub(super) fn schedule_task|pub(super) fn schedule_option_task_without_yield|fn schedule_local|fn push_remote_task" tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs'

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"pub(crate) fn spawn\\(|pub(crate) unsafe fn spawn_local\\(|pub(crate) fn block_on\\(\" tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/mod.rs tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/handle.rs tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/mod.rs"
baseline.answer:
I focused on the runtime/task-execution path. Tokio’s central flow is really two public entry points that converge on the same scheduler core: startup via `#[tokio::main]` and task submission via `tokio::spawn`.

**Flow**
- Public surface: [`tokio/src/lib.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/lib.rs#L676) reexports `tokio_macros::main`, and [`tokio/src/lib.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/lib.rs#L577) reexports `task::spawn`.
- Macro expansion: [`tokio-macros/src/entry.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio-macros/src/entry.rs#L471) chooses `Builder::new_current_thread()` or `Builder::new_multi_thread()`, then around [`tokio-macros/src/entry.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio-macros/src/entry.rs#L516) emits `.enable_all().build().expect(...).block_on(body)`.
- Runtime construction: [`tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs#L261) and [`tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs#L276) select the scheduler flavor; [`tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs#L376) enables drivers; [`tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs#L1072) builds the `Runtime`; the real assembly happens in [`build_current_thread_runtime_components`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs#L1674) and [`build_threaded_runtime`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs#L2003).
- Runtime entry: [`tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L174) shows `Runtime::new()` is just `Builder::new_multi_thread().enable_all().build()`. The main execution path is [`Runtime::spawn`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L239), [`Runtime::block_on`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L340), and [`Runtime::enter`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L421).
- Handle-level execution: [`tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs#L197) forwards `Handle::spawn`; [`tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs#L342) drives `Handle::block_on`; both use the TLS runtime context.
- Task submission: [`tokio/src/task/spawn.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/task/spawn.rs#L174) is the public `tokio::spawn`; around [`tokio/src/task/spawn.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/task/spawn.rs#L211) it grabs the current runtime from TLS and delegates to the scheduler handle.
- Runtime context/TLS: [`tokio/src/runtime/context.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context.rs#L191) exposes the scheduler-scoped TLS access; [`tokio/src/runtime/context/current.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context/current.rs#L33) is the `with_current`/`try_set_current` path; [`tokio/src/runtime/context/runtime.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context/runtime.rs#L32) is the `enter_runtime` gate that prevents nested runtime blocking.
- Scheduler seam: [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/mod.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/mod.rs#L32) is the abstraction boundary. The current-thread implementation’s `schedule` is in [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs#L702), and the multi-thread side routes through [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/handle.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/handle.rs#L110) and [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs#L1341).
- “Real work” loop: the current-thread executor runs through [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs#L203); the multi-thread worker loop is in [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs#L595) and task polling is concentrated in [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs#L641).

**What To Read Next**
- Read [`tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L97) first if you want the public runtime API plus shutdown semantics in one place.
- Read [`tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs#L261) next if your change affects runtime configuration or feature flags.
- Read [`tokio/src/runtime/context.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context.rs#L1) plus [`tokio/src/runtime/context/current.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context/current.rs#L1) if your change affects `tokio::spawn`, `Handle::current`, or nesting/enter behavior.
- Read [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs#L1) if you want the simplest end-to-end execution path.
- Read [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs#L1) if your change touches fairness, stealing, wakeups, or worker parking.
- Read [`tokio/src/task/spawn.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/task/spawn.rs#L174) if your change is about task submission, caller location, or spawn preconditions.

**Tests**
- [`tests-integration/tests/macros_main.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tests-integration/tests/macros_main.rs#L1) is the best first read for `#[tokio::main]` expansion behavior.
- [`tests-build/tests/pass/macros_main_return.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tests-build/tests/pass/macros_main_return.rs#L1) and [`tests-build/tests/pass/macros_main_loop.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tests-build/tests/pass/macros_main_loop.rs#L1) show compile-time coverage for macro output shape.
- [`tokio/tests/time_rt.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/tests/time_rt.rs#L58) covers the runtime/timer behavior on both current-thread and multi-thread runtimes.
- [`tokio/tests/time_panic.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/tests/time_panic.rs#L1) is the panic surface for time/runtime misuse.
- [`tokio/tests/task_hooks.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/tests/task_hooks.rs#L14) is the best read for spawn, terminate, and caller-location propagation.
- [`tokio/tests/task_panic.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/tests/task_panic.rs#L16) covers panic boundaries and where caller attribution is expected.
- [`tokio/src/runtime/tests/task.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/tests/task.rs#L199) exercises scheduler/task lifecycle behavior inside the runtime crate.
- [`tokio/src/runtime/tests/inject.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/tests/inject.rs#L4) and [`tokio/src/runtime/tests/queue.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/tests/queue.rs#L32) are the queue/injection invariants for the scheduler internals.

If you want, I can do a second pass on just one branch next:
1. `#[tokio::main]` expansion and runtime bootstrap
2. `tokio::spawn` and task scheduling
3. current-thread vs multi-thread worker internals
